name: Art
description: Famous paintings, sculptures, artists, and museums

questions.q: Who painted the Mona Lisa, also known as La Gioconda?

source.notes: Painted c. 1503–1519; hangs in the Louvre in Paris.

questions.q: 'The Starry Night' (1889) was painted by which Dutch Post-Impressionist?

source.notes: Painted from memory at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum.

questions.q: From what material was Michelangelo's statue of David carved?

source.notes: Carved from a single block of Carrara marble between 1501 and 1504.

questions.q: Frida Kahlo, famous for her surrealist self-portraits, was from which country?

source.notes: Born in Coyoacán, Mexico in 1907.

questions.q: What is the first name of Norwegian artist Munch, who painted 'The Scream'?

source.notes: Edvard Munch created several versions between 1893 and 1910.

questions.q: Who painted 'The Last Supper' on the wall of a Milan convent refectory?

source.notes: Painted 1495–1498 at Santa Maria delle Grazie in Milan.

questions.q: The Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum, with its famous spiral interior, is located in which city?

source.notes: On Fifth Avenue in Manhattan; designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, opened 1959.

questions.q: Pablo Picasso's mural-sized anti-war painting 'Guernica' was created in response to an event during which decade?

source.notes: Painted in 1937 after the bombing of the Basque town of Guernica during the Spanish Civil War.

questions.q: How many figures appear in Grant Wood's famous painting 'American Gothic'?

source.notes: A man holding a pitchfork and a woman in front of a white farmhouse (1930).

questions.q: Which Spanish Surrealist, famed for a flamboyant mustache, painted 'The Persistence of Memory' with its melting clocks?

source.notes: Painted 1931; now at MoMA in New York.

questions.q: Which French Impressionist produced the series of 'Water Lilies' paintings at his garden in Giverny?

source.notes: About 250 oil paintings depicting his flower garden at Giverny.

questions.q: Which French artist's pointillist masterpiece 'A Sunday on La Grande Jatte' uses thousands of tiny dots of color?

source.notes: Painted 1884–1886; a foundational Neo-Impressionist work.

questions.q: Which Italian Renaissance painter depicted the goddess of love emerging from the sea on a shell in 'The Birth of Venus'?

source.notes: Painted c. 1485; now at the Uffizi in Florence.

questions.q: Johannes Vermeer, painter of 'Girl with a Pearl Earring', came from which country?

source.notes: Born and worked in Delft during the Dutch Golden Age.

questions.q: Which Dutch master painted the massive group portrait known as 'The Night Watch' in 1642?

source.notes: Full title 'Militia Company of District II under the Command of Captain Frans Banninck Cocq'; hangs at the Rijksmuseum.

questions.q: Which British sculptor created the towering 'Angel of the North' in Gateshead?

source.notes: Unveiled 1998; 20 metres tall with a 54-metre wingspan.

questions.q: Which French Post-Impressionist painter left Paris for Tahiti in 1891 to create works inspired by Polynesian life?

source.notes: Gauguin spent most of his later years in French Polynesia.

questions.q: Andy Warhol's breakthrough 1962 exhibition featured paintings of which everyday product?

source.notes: Shown at the Ferus Gallery in Los Angeles in July 1962.

questions.q: What do the initials 'L.S.' stand for in the name of Salford painter L.S. Lowry?

source.notes: Famous for matchstick figures in industrial northern English scenes.

questions.q: Gustav Klimt's gold-leafed painting 'The Kiss' is displayed in which European capital?

source.notes: Held at the Österreichische Galerie Belvedere.

questions.q: The ancient Greek sculpture 'Venus de Milo' depicts which goddess?

source.notes: Known to the Greeks as Aphrodite (Roman: Venus); now at the Louvre.

questions.q: Leonardo da Vinci's 'Salvator Mundi' became the most expensive painting ever sold at auction in 2017, fetching approximately what price?

source.notes: Sold for $450.3 million at Christie's New York in November 2017.
